$comment: ADR-ACT-0237 — Universal Service Foundation registry. DATA ONLY. This file does NOT assert that any capability is implemented; the `status` field is the honest, source-traced state at the date below. Single source of truth for docs/evidence/platform/universal-service-foundation-matrix.md. Validated by tools/architecture/validate-universal-foundation. Do not collapse `compose container exists` into a delivered status.

capabilities.name: Tenant identity (record + FQDN)
capabilities.purpose: A tenant is a first-class isolated entity with a stable record and host identity.
capabilities.dataIsolation: row-level via tenant schema; FQDN host authority
capabilities.readinessModel: tenant-context readiness kind (always ready once resolved)
capabilities.productionBlockers: none for core record
capabilities.notes: Foundation already delivered; listed for completeness.

capabilities.name: User identity + tenant membership
capabilities.purpose: Users belong to tenants with roles and membership status.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none
capabilities.notes: Delivered control-plane capability.

capabilities.name: End-user profile + preferences self-service
capabilities.purpose: End users manage their own profile, preferences, notification settings, sessions, and devices.
capabilities.composeSupport: keycloak account console (not surfaced); profile-configuration port scaffold only
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Keycloak Account console + Postgres-backed profile-configuration adapter
capabilities.tenantIsolation: user belongs to one realm; preferences tenant+user scoped
capabilities.productionBlockers: no persistence adapter; no route; no UI
capabilities.notes: packages/profile-configuration is an in-memory port scaffold; not wired.

capabilities.name: API keys / personal access tokens
capabilities.purpose: Programmatic credentials for users and service accounts.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Keycloak service accounts / client credentials + custom token store
capabilities.productionBlockers: full capability absent
capabilities.notes: Secret handling must reuse write-only + redaction pattern from auth credentials.

capabilities.name: Tenant groups
capabilities.purpose: Group membership for bulk role assignment.
capabilities.productionBlockers: no admin UI
capabilities.notes: capability-registry marks tenant_groups partial; routes exist, UI does not.

capabilities.name: Sub-organisations
capabilities.purpose: Nested organisational units within a tenant.
capabilities.productionBlockers: no admin UI
capabilities.notes: capability-registry marks tenant_suborgs partial.

capabilities.name: RBAC (roles + permissions)
capabilities.purpose: Role-based access for tenant members.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none
capabilities.notes: Delivered.

capabilities.name: ABAC / Policy Decision Point
capabilities.purpose: Attribute-based, runtime-configurable authorization decisions.
capabilities.composeSupport: keycloak Authorization Services (UMA 2.0 PEP) — authorisation-runtime is real
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Keycloak UMA (current); OPA/Cedar adapter as an optional external PDP
capabilities.productionBlockers: no general ABAC attribute model beyond UMA scopes; the entitlement step of the chain is delivered (Phase 1) but real quota enforcement is Phase 2
capabilities.notes: Decision: keep Keycloak UMA as PDP; do NOT add OPA unless an attribute-policy need is proven.

capabilities.name: Delegated administration roles
capabilities.purpose: Tenant admins grant scoped admin rights to others.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Keycloak fine-grained admin + custom scope mapping
capabilities.productionBlockers: needs ADR before implementation
capabilities.notes: capability-registry marks delegated_admin_roles deferred; ADR-0058 to define.

capabilities.name: Entitlement engine
capabilities.purpose: Resolve what a tenant/plan is entitled to use; gate features by entitlement, not just flags.
capabilities.composeSupport: feature flags exist but are not plan-linked entitlements
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: custom entitlement table + OpenMeter/Lago plan link
capabilities.productionBlockers: entitlement substrate is locally proven (live Postgres RLS + live BFF route handlers); real quota enforcement is still Phase 2 (only a hook); billing/metering not delivered
capabilities.notes: Phase 1 + 1.5 (ADR-ACT-0254/0255): entitlement engine LIVE-proven against Compose Postgres (RLS isolation — migration 023 fixed a real bypass-predicate flaw caught by the proof) and live BFF route handlers. Deny-by-default + audit-before-change + no-self-grant + evaluation chain + quota HOOK (Phase-2 only). Operator console picks a tenant via a lookup Select (no raw UUID). Billing/metering/real quotas not delivered.

capabilities.name: Support-mode / break-glass access
capabilities.purpose: Time-boxed, audited operator access into a tenant.
capabilities.productionBlockers: no approval workflow; host-origin escalation deferred (per bedrock evidence)
capabilities.notes: Escalation on tenant hosts deferred pending an ADR (bedrock hardening evidence).

capabilities.name: Audit of privileged access
capabilities.purpose: Durable, tenant-scoped audit of admin and privileged operations.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none; retention policy not yet enforced
capabilities.notes: Delivered.

capabilities.name: Platform login + session
capabilities.purpose: Browser login, HTTP-only cookie session, forward-auth.
capabilities.tenantIsolation: FQDN-scoped session; realm per tenant model
capabilities.productionBlockers: real IdP login simulation blocked (see real-idp-proof)
capabilities.notes: Delivered/proven against mock-oidc.

capabilities.name: IdP brokering + OIDC provider management
capabilities.purpose: Per-tenant external IdP federation (OIDC).
capabilities.productionBlockers: SAML not supported (OIDC only); claim/group mapping partial
capabilities.notes: OIDC delivered; SAML is a gap.

capabilities.name: Claim mapping + group/role mapping
capabilities.purpose: Map external IdP claims/groups to platform roles.
capabilities.productionBlockers: real-IdP mapping proof blocked (ADR-ACT-0220)
capabilities.notes: Machinery present; proof blocked on real IdP.

capabilities.name: Real IdP login simulation
capabilities.purpose: Prove end-to-end login against a real external IdP.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: none local; requires real Google/Microsoft/Okta tenant
capabilities.productionBlockers: no real IdP available in local stack
capabilities.notes: Explicitly blocked; mock-oidc fixture is not a substitute.

capabilities.name: MFA + session policy + lockout
capabilities.purpose: Configurable MFA, session lifetime, account lockout/recovery.
capabilities.productionBlockers: account lockout/recovery surface not exposed; MFA-required login E2E deferred
capabilities.notes: MFA/session writable + proven; lockout/recovery is a gap.

capabilities.name: Configuration registry + history
capabilities.purpose: Typed per-tenant config with env defaults, override, reset, audit.
capabilities.auditEvents: config change audited; tenant.config.changed webhook event
capabilities.productionBlockers: full point-in-time rollback partial (reset-to-default only)
capabilities.notes: Delivered; rollback history is a future enhancement.

capabilities.name: Branding + theming
capabilities.purpose: Per-tenant theme tokens and branding.
capabilities.productionBlockers: branding capability marked partial in registry
capabilities.notes: Theme delivered; rich branding partial.

capabilities.name: Custom domains, DNS ownership, TLS, canonical
capabilities.purpose: Tenant-owned vanity domains with verified DNS, TLS, canonical redirect.
capabilities.composeSupport: caddy on-demand TLS; DNS challenge; cloudflare adapter
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Caddy + custom DNS challenge (composed); Cloudflare adapter for prod
capabilities.tenantIsolation: domain claimed by one tenant; cross-tenant conflict rejected pre-token
capabilities.productionBlockers: public DNS verification blocked locally; canonical redirect cutover not proven
capabilities.notes: Machinery live; public DNS + redirect cutover are honest gaps (bedrock evidence).

capabilities.name: Write-only secret settings
capabilities.purpose: Operators set credentials that are never read back.
capabilities.productionBlockers: no central secrets manager (Vault) — env/db only
capabilities.notes: Pattern delivered; a Vault/KMS adapter is a later production hardening.

capabilities.name: Product catalog, plans, prices
capabilities.purpose: Define sellable products, plans, and pricing.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Lago (OSS) or Kill Bill; OpenMeter for usage
capabilities.productionBlockers: no billing engine composed or adapted
capabilities.notes: Discovery first: Lago vs OpenMeter vs Kill Bill vs custom ledger.

capabilities.name: Subscriptions, invoices, payment methods, dunning
capabilities.purpose: Lifecycle of paid subscriptions including invoicing and dunning.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Lago/Kill Bill (OSS); payment gateway is prod-external adapter
capabilities.dataIsolation: provider + local mirror; PCI handled by gateway
capabilities.productionBlockers: payment provider is paid/external; no local payment proof possible
capabilities.notes: Payment capture is the only place a paid external adapter is unavoidable; everything else proven locally.

capabilities.name: Usage metering + meter event ingestion
capabilities.purpose: Ingest usage events and aggregate into meters.
capabilities.composeSupport: built-in Postgres meter_events (migration 024); ClickHouse available for the later provider
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: built-in Postgres meter store; OpenMeter/ClickHouse provider behind MeteringRepository is Phase 2.5
capabilities.dataIsolation: RLS + idempotency unique (org, meter, idempotency key)
capabilities.auditEvents: n/a (meter events are the data; quota changes are audited)
capabilities.productionBlockers: built-in Postgres meter store live-proven; high-volume ClickHouse/OpenMeter provider is Phase 2.5 (behind the port)
capabilities.notes: Phase 2 (ADR-ACT-0256): built-in Postgres metering — RLS-isolated, idempotent, windowed aggregation, entitlement-gated ingestion — live-proven (proof:metering). ClickHouse/OpenMeter providerisation is Phase 2.5 behind MeteringRepository.

capabilities.name: Quota enforcement
capabilities.purpose: Enforce per-tenant limits derived from plan/entitlement.
capabilities.composeSupport: built-in tenant_quotas (migration 024) + meter aggregation; no Redis required for the proof
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: built-in quota check using entitlements + windowed meter aggregation
capabilities.dataIsolation: RLS; usage aggregated from RLS-scoped meter_events
capabilities.permissionModel: enforced at BFF after permission + entitlement; tenant.metering.read / platform.quotas.read|write
capabilities.productionBlockers: live-proven against Postgres; denial returns a typed 403 (429 semantics a later refinement)
capabilities.notes: Phase 2 (ADR-ACT-0256): real quota enforcement (entitlement → usage-vs-limit over a window) replaces the Phase-1 no-op hook; entitlement is checked BEFORE quota; quota changes audited; live-proven (proof:quota-enforcement).

capabilities.name: Relational storage + migrations + RLS
capabilities.purpose: Transactional data with schema lifecycle and tenant isolation.
capabilities.permissionModel: server-only; no direct DB from React
capabilities.productionBlockers: none for core; PITR/residency separate rows
capabilities.notes: Delivered/proven foundation.

capabilities.name: Backup + restore
capabilities.purpose: Recoverable backups of tenant data.
capabilities.dataIsolation: umask 077 chmod 600; refuses non-dev/test without ALLOW_BACKUP_ENV
capabilities.productionBlockers: no scheduled/offsite backup; no production restore drill
capabilities.notes: Local scripts proven; production backup/restore lifecycle is the gap.

capabilities.name: PITR, retention, legal hold, data residency
capabilities.purpose: Point-in-time recovery, retention enforcement, legal hold, residency controls.
capabilities.composeSupport: none (Sentry has its own 90d cleanup only)
capabilities.productionBlockers: no PITR, retention, legal hold, or residency machinery
capabilities.notes: Compliance-driven; pairs with data governance.

capabilities.name: Data governance: catalog, lineage, classification, PII, DSR/GDPR
capabilities.purpose: Catalog, classify, trace, and satisfy data-subject requests.
capabilities.sharedPerEnv: shared (engineering metadata, not tenant runtime data) — requires partitioning proof
capabilities.tenantIsolation: catalog is metadata; DSR workflows tenant-scoped
capabilities.dataIsolation: metadata only; DSR acts on per-tenant data
capabilities.productionBlockers: no catalog, lineage, classification, PII discovery, or DSR workflow
capabilities.notes: Catalog may be shared if metadata-only; DSR must be per-tenant.

capabilities.name: Tenant data import / export
capabilities.purpose: Bulk import and export of tenant data.
capabilities.productionBlockers: absent; pairs with tenant deletion/portability
capabilities.notes: Needed for tenant offboarding + GDPR portability.

capabilities.name: Search + indexing (product search)
capabilities.purpose: Tenant-scoped, permission-aware full-text and filtered search.
capabilities.composeSupport: search-runtime is a PORT-ONLY scaffold; no engine composed
capabilities.tenantIsolation: per-tenant index or tenant filter + permission filter
capabilities.productionBlockers: no engine, adapter, route, or UI; Loki log-search is NOT product search
capabilities.notes: Meilisearch/Typesense favoured for low compose burden + tenant isolation.

capabilities.name: Object storage + tenant prefixes + signed URLs
capabilities.purpose: S3-compatible storage with tenant isolation and signed access.
capabilities.composeSupport: minio (per-env); storage-runtime real port; tenant-storage usecase
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: MinIO (composed); real S3 adapter for prod (ADR-ACT-0223)
capabilities.productionBlockers: no file CRUD API/UI, no quotas, lifecycle, AV scan, or legal hold
capabilities.notes: Readiness + isolation proven; storage product (browser, quotas, lifecycle) is the gap.

capabilities.name: Event bus, durable queues, DLQ, redrive
capabilities.purpose: Internal eventing, durable job queues, dead-letter + redrive.
capabilities.composeSupport: redis; queue-runtime port scaffold; webhook-delivery worker real
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Redis Streams (composed) or NATS/Redpanda if scale demands
capabilities.sharedPerEnv: per-env (Sentry Kafka is Sentry-only, not the platform bus)
capabilities.productionBlockers: outbound webhooks delivered; general internal event bus / pub-sub topics absent
capabilities.notes: Webhook substrate is real and proven; generalise to internal eventing carefully.

capabilities.name: Workflow engine, scheduled jobs, approvals
capabilities.purpose: Durable orchestration of long-running and scheduled work with visibility.
capabilities.composeSupport: single webhook worker only; no general scheduler/engine
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Temporal (heavy) or Windmill (light) — both OSS local
capabilities.productionBlockers: no engine, scheduler, approval workflow, or visibility
capabilities.notes: Windmill favoured for low burden; Temporal if durable long-running guarantees needed.

capabilities.name: Notification delivery + preferences + channels
capabilities.purpose: Deliver notifications across email/in-app/push/SMS with per-user preferences.
capabilities.composeSupport: notification-runtime PORT-ONLY scaffold; email-runtime + mailpit exist for email
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Novu (OSS) or ntfy; local SMTP via Mailpit; webhook adapter
capabilities.bffContract: email-sender routes only; no general notification API
capabilities.productionBlockers: only transactional email delivered; multi-channel + preferences absent
capabilities.notes: Email channel proven via Brevo/SMTP/Mailpit; Novu for in-app/push if needed.

capabilities.name: Background workers / job runner
capabilities.purpose: Run scheduled and async work reliably with retries.
capabilities.bffContract: platform services readiness exposes worker status
capabilities.productionBlockers: single worker; in-memory heartbeat (no persistence); no general job runner or scaling
capabilities.notes: Heartbeat persistence deferred per operations-cockpit evidence.

capabilities.name: Serverless / function runtime
capabilities.purpose: Tenant- or platform-authored functions on demand.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Windmill scripts or OpenFaaS (OSS) — only if a real need emerges
capabilities.productionBlockers: no runtime; high security surface
capabilities.notes: Deferred until a concrete product need; large security surface.

capabilities.name: Runtime secrets management
capabilities.purpose: Central, audited secret storage and rotation.
capabilities.composeSupport: env + db encryption; LocalStack secretsmanager is a MOCK only
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Vault OSS (local dev) or a local KMS abstraction
capabilities.productionBlockers: no central secrets manager; secrets via env/db
capabilities.notes: LocalStack secretsmanager is mock-only; do not treat as production substrate.

capabilities.name: Logs (aggregation + tenant-scoped search)
capabilities.purpose: Structured log ingestion and tenant-scoped search.
capabilities.productionBlockers: Loki filesystem storage (prod needs S3 backend)
capabilities.notes: Delivered/proven for logs.

capabilities.name: Metrics + traces
capabilities.purpose: Metric and trace collection with a queryable backend.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Prometheus + Tempo (OSS) behind the existing OTEL collector
capabilities.productionBlockers: OTEL collector ingests but no metrics/trace backend or dashboards
capabilities.notes: Collector is the seam; add Prometheus+Tempo backends.

capabilities.name: Alerting, incident management, on-call, status page
capabilities.purpose: Alert rules, incident lifecycle, escalation, and public status.
capabilities.composeSupport: grafana exists but no Alertmanager; no incident tool
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Grafana Alerting + Alertmanager (OSS); status page via custom + readiness API
capabilities.sharedPerEnv: shared (engineering ops) with env labels; tenant comms separate
capabilities.tenantIsolation: alerts tagged by env/tenant; tenant comms scoped
capabilities.bffContract: platform services readiness (internal); no incident API
capabilities.productionBlockers: no alert rules, notification channels, SLOs, incident lifecycle, on-call, or status page
capabilities.notes: Readiness != alerting. Grafana running != incident management.

capabilities.name: Internal service catalog + readiness
capabilities.purpose: Inventory of platform services with honest health/readiness.
capabilities.dataIsolation: no secrets in payload; allowlisted console URLs
capabilities.productionBlockers: point-in-time only; no history
capabilities.notes: Delivered/proven; foundation for alerting/incident.

capabilities.name: Code quality + secret + dependency scanning
capabilities.purpose: Engineering-quality and supply-chain scanning.
capabilities.composeSupport: sonarqube (shared, react-sonar); gitleaks in CI; semgrep rules
capabilities.sharedPerEnv: shared (engineering quality, not tenant runtime data)
capabilities.productionBlockers: dependency scanning not yet a hard gate
capabilities.notes: Sonar legitimately shared: it holds engineering-quality data, not tenant runtime data, so there is no tenant data to leak across environments. Sentry is shared errors-only with env/tenant tagging and retention boundaries.

capabilities.name: Compliance reports, access reviews, evidence packs
capabilities.purpose: Generate compliance evidence and run periodic access/role reviews.
capabilities.bffContract: audit read only; no review/report API
capabilities.productionBlockers: no compliance report generation, access-review or role-review workflow
capabilities.notes: Audit trail is the raw material; reviews/reports are missing.

capabilities.name: Tenant isolation proof
capabilities.purpose: Demonstrable cross-tenant isolation across data and routing.
capabilities.composeSupport: RLS + FQDN routing + storage isolation probe
capabilities.productionBlockers: shared services (Sentry/Sonar/Grafana/Mailpit) have NO per-tenant isolation — global-only by policy
capabilities.notes: Core data/routing isolation proven; shared-tool isolation gaps are explicit in clickthrough policy.

capabilities.name: Webhooks (developer-facing)
capabilities.purpose: Outbound event subscriptions with signing, delivery, redrive, metrics.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none
capabilities.notes: Delivered/proven; cornerstone of developer platform.

capabilities.name: API docs, developer portal, SDKs, rate limits
capabilities.purpose: Self-serve developer experience: docs, portal, SDKs, rate-limited keys, sandbox.
capabilities.composeSupport: openapi.json baseline; GraphQL primary; no portal
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Redocly/Swagger UI (docs) + Backstage or Kong OSS (portal/gateway)
capabilities.productionBlockers: no portal, SDK gen, rate limits, sandbox/test mode, or OpenAPI explorer; OpenAPI drift incomplete
capabilities.notes: OpenAPI baseline exists; portal/SDK/rate-limit are gaps.

capabilities.name: Mock providers (dev/test)
capabilities.purpose: Deterministic mocks for external dependencies in dev/test.
capabilities.productionBlockers: FORBIDDEN in production
capabilities.notes: Must never be treated as production substrate; forbidden-in-production.

capabilities.name: Tenant lifecycle: provision, suspend, delete, export
capabilities.purpose: Full lifecycle management of a tenant including suspension and deletion.
capabilities.dataIsolation: delete must purge schema + storage + realm
capabilities.productionBlockers: no suspend, delete, or export; deletion must coordinate data + storage + realm + DSR
capabilities.notes: Deletion/offboarding couples with data governance + import-export.

capabilities.name: Support tickets, customer health, announcements
capabilities.purpose: Support desk, tenant health signals, incident communication, announcements.
capabilities.localFreeCandidate: Zammad or Chatwoot (OSS) for tickets; built-in announcements
capabilities.sharedPerEnv: shared (support desk) with tenant tagging + access control
capabilities.dataIsolation: support tool DB; tenant tag mandatory
capabilities.productionBlockers: no ticketing, health, comms, or announcements
capabilities.notes: If shared, tenant tagging + retention + access control are mandatory.

capabilities.name: Universal Service Foundation scope + principles
capabilities.purpose: Define what the foundation is and is not; prevent fake-readiness and scope creep.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none (decision artifact)
capabilities.notes: This deliverable. Status reflects the planning artifact, not the foundation.

capabilities.name: Build-versus-compose decision framework
capabilities.purpose: A repeatable rubric for build/compose/adapter/defer/reject per capability.
capabilities.productionBlockers: none
capabilities.notes: Framework doc delivered in this pass.

capabilities.name: Service catalog + provider integration model
capabilities.purpose: How services are registered, classified, probed, and adapted to providers.
capabilities.productionBlockers: catalog v2 seam + provider registry + no-mock-in-production invariant delivered (static seed); full generalisation of platform-services/service-clickthrough into the catalog is incremental
capabilities.notes: Phase 1 (ADR-ACT-0254): service catalog v2 seam + provider registry port + buildServiceCatalog filtering + forbiddenProvidersForEnvironment invariant, proven via proof:service-catalog-registry. Generalising the existing registries fully is incremental.

capabilities.name: Environment-specific vs shared service model
capabilities.purpose: Classify every service per-env/shared/mock/forbidden with isolation guarantees.
capabilities.composeSupport: compose profiles already encode much of this
capabilities.tenantIsolation: shared services require tagging + access + retention
capabilities.dataIsolation: leakage analysis required for shared services
capabilities.productionBlockers: none (decision artifact)
capabilities.notes: Classification doc delivered in this pass.

delivery.$comment: ADR-ACT-0252 — delivery spine. DATA ONLY. Encodes phase assignment + the dependency graph that orders implementation. `blocks` is NOT stored here; it is derived as the inverse of `dependsOn` by the validator and the dependencies doc so the two can never drift. `track` records whether a row is already delivered (excluded from ordering), planned, blocked, deferred, mock, or a planning artifact. Human views: universal-service-foundation-delivery-dependencies.md (graph) and universal-service-foundation-implementation-roadmap.md (phases). Validated by tools/architecture/validate-universal-foundation.

phaseTitles.delivered: Already delivered or partially delivered (not on the new roadmap)
phaseTitles.deferred: Deferred until a concrete product need is proven

requiredDependencyTruths.truth: service catalog precedes generic provider composition
service catalog precedes generic provider composition
service catalog precedes generic provider composition
service catalog precedes generic provider composition
API keys precede developer portal and rate limits
data governance precedes tenant deletion/export and DSR
backup/recovery precedes production tenant lifecycle confidence

requiredUiPrecedence.truth: search indexing precedes product search UI

phaseGates.$comment: A phase's planned capabilities may not advance to a delivered-ish status until its gating ADRs are Accepted or implementation-ready. Enforced by validate-universal-foundation (ADR-ACT-0253).

dependencies.riskIfDelayed: Without a scope boundary the foundation drifts into fake readiness and scope creep.
Build/compose decisions made ad hoc; accidental rewrites of mature capabilities.
Unsafe shared services composed without an isolation/leakage analysis.
Every composed service integrates ad hoc; no consistent provider-adapter catalog.
Billing, quotas, and paid feature gating all stall; flags cannot become plan-linked entitlements.
Delegated admin and scoped API keys cannot be modelled.
Usage billing and quotas have no signal source.
No limit enforcement; abuse and cost-overrun risk.
No programmatic access; developer portal and rate limits blocked.
No self-serve developer experience; rate limits unenforceable; OpenAPI drift stays unguarded.
No product search; the SearchPort scaffold stays unwired.
No internal eventing backbone; workflow engine and multi-channel notifications blocked.
Single in-memory worker cannot scale or persist heartbeat.
No durable orchestration, approvals, or scheduled jobs; DSR/import-export/dunning blocked.
No end-user self-service; notification preferences blocked.
Only transactional email exists; incident escalation and user preferences blocked.
Routes exist but no bulk role-assignment UI.
Routes exist but no nested-org UI.
Tenant admins cannot delegate scoped admin rights.
OTEL collector ingests but there is no backend; SLOs and alerting blocked.
No alert rules, on-call, incident lifecycle, or public status page.
Local scripts only; no production recovery confidence; tenant lifecycle unsafe.
No point-in-time recovery, retention, legal hold, or residency controls.
No portability/offboarding; DSR and tenant deletion blocked.
No DSR/GDPR; tenant deletion unsafe; compliance gap.
No access reviews or compliance evidence packs.
No safe suspend/delete; compliance and offboarding risk.
Secrets remain env/db only; no central rotation or audit; LocalStack secretsmanager is mock-only.
No sellable catalog; monetization blocked.
No monetization. Payment capture is the only production-external paid dependency and cannot be proven locally end-to-end.
Storage stays readiness-only; no file product, quotas, lifecycle, AV scan, or legal hold.
No ticketing, customer health, incident comms, or announcements.
n/a — foundation, locally proven.
n/a — delivered.
n/a — delivered.
n/a — delivered; retention enforcement is a phase-8 follow-up.
n/a — locally proven against mock-oidc.
n/a — OIDC delivered; SAML is a documented gap.
Machinery present; real-IdP mapping proof is blocked (ADR-ACT-0220).
Enterprise SSO remains unproven against a real external IdP; mock-oidc cannot substitute.
MFA/session writable and proven; lockout/recovery surface + MFA-required E2E deferred.
n/a — delivered; full rollback history is a future enhancement.
Theme delivered; rich branding partial.
Machinery live; public DNS verification + canonical cutover unproven locally.
Pattern delivered; a Vault/KMS adapter is the phase-8 runtime-secrets follow-up.
n/a — foundation, locally proven.
n/a — locally proven; prod needs an S3 Loki backend.
n/a — locally proven; the seed for the service-catalog-provider-model.
Sonar/gitleaks/semgrep present; dependency scanning not yet a hard gate.
n/a — core isolation proven; shared-tool gaps explicit in clickthrough policy.
n/a — locally proven; the seed for the event/queue substrate.
Audited support-session present; approval workflow + host-origin escalation deferred.
None — explicitly deferred until a concrete product need; large security surface.
n/a — forbidden in production; must never be a production substrate.
